heading: Make an API (Application Programming Interface) Request
body:
The Atlas Administration API uses one of two authentication methods to authenticate requests:
service account or API (Application Programming Interface) keys. You'll need the keys or secret that you saved when
[create a new one](create-org-api-key) to
complete the following procedures.

All Atlas Administration API endpoints have the following base URL:

`https://cloud.mongodb.com/api/atlas/<version>`

> **Important:** MongoDB uses HTTPS URLs to increase security. Using HTTP URLs will return a 301 status code.

> **See Also:** 
>
> - [Atlas Administration API Specification](https://mongodb.com/docs/atlas/reference/api-resources-spec)
>
> - [Atlas Administration API Error Codes](atlas-api-error-codes)

Alternatively, you can use any tool that supports [OpenAPI v3
specification](https://spec.openapis.org/oas/v3.0.1) to generate code
samples or mock servers. For example, you can import
the [Atlas Admin API Specification](atlas/reference/api-resources-spec)
into [Postman](https://www.postman.com/) to generate `curl`
commands.

> **Warning:** Using an HTTP URL with Postman will return a 301 status code as expected. However, in this scenario, Postman may automatically retry the request with HTTPS, but simultaneously strip the header and body from the retry request. This will return a 401 status code instead of 301, making it difficult to determine why the request failed.

To use Postman to generate `curl` commands:
